# Kool Codez — Full Reference

> Frontend engineering by Lucio D'Alessandro. Open source React and TypeScript libraries plus long form technical writing on frontend architecture, rendering performance, WebSockets, and maintainable UI systems.

## About

Kool Codez is the personal engineering site of Lucio D'Alessandro, a senior frontend contractor with a decade of production React and TypeScript experience. Work includes UI rebuilds at a global investment bank, trading grid systems handling real time market data, and a catalog of open source libraries covering WebSockets, accessible dropdowns, gesture driven navigation, and headless forms.

- Site: https://koolcodez.com
- GitHub: https://github.com/luciodale
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/lucio-d-alessandro-38639bb6/
- Email: lucio.dalessa@gmail.com

## Libraries

### react-socket

A batteries included React library for real time WebSocket communication.

- Package: `@luciodale/react-socket` on npm
- Install: `npm install @luciodale/react-socket`
- Repository: https://github.com/luciodale/react-socket
- Project page: https://koolcodez.com/projects/react-socket
- Language: TypeScript
- License: MIT

Features
- Type safe hooks for subscribing to and sending WebSocket messages.
- Automatic reconnection with backoff.
- Optimistic updates for responsive UIs.
- Offline message queuing so messages sent while disconnected are flushed on reconnect.
- Zero boilerplate setup for typical use cases.

Ideal for
- Streaming LLM clients (OpenAI Responses API WebSocket mode, Gemini Live API).
- Real time trading UIs.
- Chat, collaboration, and agentic workflows with many tool calls.

### react-searchable-dropdown

A high performance, virtualized dropdown and combobox component for React.

- Package: `@luciodale/react-searchable-dropdown` on npm
- Install: `npm install @luciodale/react-searchable-dropdown`
- Repository: https://github.com/luciodale/react-searchable-dropdown
- Project page: https://koolcodez.com/projects/react-searchable-dropdown
- Language: TypeScript
- License: MIT

Features
- Single and multi selection modes.
- Async search with debouncing.
- Fuzzy matching out of the box.
- Custom rendering for options and selected state.
- Create new option inline.
- Virtualized list rendering for large datasets.
- Zero runtime dependencies.
- Accessible keyboard navigation and ARIA support.

Ideal for
- Country and currency pickers.
- Large entity lookups (users, tickers, products).
- Tagging and multi select workflows.

### swipe-bar

A gesture driven sidebar component that brings native app fluidity to the web.

- Package: `@luciodale/swipe-bar` on npm
- Install: `npm install @luciodale/swipe-bar`
- Repository: https://github.com/luciodale/swipe-bar
- Project page: https://koolcodez.com/projects/swipe-bar
- Language: TypeScript
- License: MIT

Features
- Touch and mouse gesture support.
- Spring physics for natural feeling motion.
- Tiny bundle footprint.
- Works without external animation dependencies.
- Mobile first, designed for web apps that want to feel native.

Ideal for
- Mobile drawers and navigation sidebars.
- PWA navigation that competes with native feel.

### fork

Headless form management for Re-frame and Reagent (ClojureScript).

- Package: `fork` on Clojars
- Repository: https://github.com/luciodale/fork
- Project page: https://koolcodez.com/projects/fork
- Language: ClojureScript
- License: MIT

Features
- Declarative validation.
- Submission handling.
- Dirty tracking per field.
- Field state management decoupled from rendering.

## Articles

### WebSockets Are Back. The Client Side needs more support.
https://koolcodez.com/blog/websockets-are-back
Why bidirectional streaming is newly central to frontend work: OpenAI's Responses API WebSocket mode, Gemini Live API, and the gap in tooling that motivated react-socket.

### How Close Can a PWA Get to a Native App? Closer Than You Think
https://koolcodez.com/blog/how-close-can-a-pwa-get-to-a-native-app
A practical experiment pushing the web platform toward native feel, including a live demo and pragmatic findings on when a full native rewrite is unnecessary.

### Inside a 16.67 Millisecond Frame
https://koolcodez.com/blog/inside-the-frame
How rendering actually unfolds within a single frame budget, explained through a practical example.

### From Spaghetti to Simplicity
https://koolcodez.com/blog/from-spaghetti-to-simplicity
Lessons from rebuilding a legacy trading UI at a global bank: identifying the real constraints, restructuring state, and shipping without blowing the release train.

### Trading Grids: A Technical Deep Dive
https://koolcodez.com/blog/trading-grid
Patterns for production ready financial data grids that handle real time market data and complex interaction without regressing performance.

### 10 CSS Features I've Found Really Useful
https://koolcodez.com/blog/useful-css-features
Lesser known CSS properties that solve common UI problems cleanly, with working examples.

### We Badly Need Frameworks
https://koolcodez.com/blog/we-badly-need-frameworks
A balanced take on what frontend frameworks still do better than assembled toolchains, with honest tradeoffs.

## Availability

Lucio is open to senior frontend engagements, especially in fintech, developer tooling, and high polish UI work where maintainability matters. Contact via https://koolcodez.com/contacts.
